trend: High-volatility correction within a longer-term uptrend; S&P 500 trading near 6,684 as of 12 Mar, roughly 2.5% below recent highs but still ~19% higher year-on-year.
sentiment: Defensive and headline-driven. Elevated VIX around the high-20s, Iran-linked disruption in the Strait of Hormuz, and in-line February CPI at 2.4% YoY keep risk appetite fragile heading into the 17–18 Mar FOMC.
fed_policy: Data dependent — March 17–18 FOMC is widely expected to leave rates unchanged, with Powell’s guidance on oil-driven inflation and the updated dot plot the key driver for risk assets.
next_major_catalyst: US February PPI (18 Mar), FOMC statement and dot plot (18 Mar), ECB rate decision (19 Mar), weekly US jobless claims (19 Mar), and a dense earnings cluster including Micron, Williams-Sonoma, Alibaba, Accenture, and FedEx around 18–19 Mar.

strategy_adjustments.macro-volatility: Prime window — high VIX and back-to-back macro events (PPI and FOMC on 18 Mar, ECB on 19 Mar) support volatility breakouts in indices, gold, oil, and USD pairs. Reduce directional exposure immediately into FOMC; favour post-event breakout or fade setups rather than pre-positioning with leverage.
strategy_adjustments.earnings-momentum: Concentrate on high-conviction pre-earnings plays (Micron 18 Mar, Williams-Sonoma 18 Mar, Alibaba / Accenture / FedEx 19 Mar). Treat all as short holding windows with strict rules to exit before the print; no leveraged holds through earnings or FOMC.
strategy_adjustments.post-shock: Still attractive in this regime — focus on quality names that crashed on guidance or one-off headlines (Sea, MongoDB, BioNTech, Amazon). Require 1–2 days of stabilisation and confirmation above crash-day support; avoid structurally impaired stories.
strategy_adjustments.crypto: BTC-dominant environment. BTC has held around the 70k area despite macro and war shocks; keep altcoin exposure minimal and conviction low outside BTC while VIX remains elevated. Favour dip buys into clearly defined support zones with tight invalidation.
strategy_adjustments.pair-trades: Remain regime-neutral but particularly useful to reduce index beta into FOMC and Iran headlines. Favour energy-vs-airlines and quality-vs-reset tech/healthcare pairs; keep spread stops tight and expected holding periods shorter than usual.

notes: February US CPI printed in line at +2.4% YoY and +0.3% MoM, easing fears of an upside shock but doing little to offset oil-driven forward inflation risk from the US–Israel–Iran conflict and near-halt in Strait of Hormuz shipping. VIX has spiked back into the high-20s, with front-month futures around the mid-20s and up more than 25% over the past month, confirming a high-volatility regime. The S&P 500 (US500) is trading near 6,684, roughly 2.5% down year-to-date but ~19% higher year-on-year, with breadth pressured by energy shocks and rate-cut expectations being pushed further out. The March 17–18 FOMC meeting and 18 Mar PPI print are the dominant macro catalysts; ECB on 19 Mar adds a second European rates impulse. On the micro side, Micron, Williams-Sonoma, Alibaba, Accenture and FedEx all report between 18–19 Mar, creating a dense event cluster for the Earnings Momentum tab. Execution overlay: favour entries during London open and US session overlap; avoid initiating new macro trades during thin Asian hours; keep leverage at the low end of allowed ranges in front of FOMC and major Iran headlines.
